summary:
"This is a practical course that focuses on conversational English. Students will practice conversation with a native speaker teacher on a variety of topics (including sharing basic personal information and topics about Japan) to improve their speaking and listening skills and deepen their international understanding.
The goal is to improve skills (conversational techniques, pronunciation and listening) by holding conversations in English. Students will learn through small group interaction, tasks and activities focused on student-centered communication. Students will develop abilities in spoken English and deepen knowledge about cross-cultural communication. "
aims: The purpose of this course is for students to acquire the basic abilities of daily English conversation by communicating and to develop their English proficiency. English Conversation is a practical course in which students practice skills to express themselves in a foreign language and negotiate meaning in communicative conversational settings.
goals: The ability to initiate conversation and share rudimentary personal information with others in English
The ability to share cultural and culinary information about Japan as well as assisting others with the basics of living or travelling in Japan.
Practice the use of English to describe and interpret images and short videos
Develop vocabulary, both formal and localized idomatic phrases and slang

plan.text: Orientation: Course outline, expectations and goals, small group conversation
Getting to Know You: The Basics (name, age, describing yourself), small group conversation
Getting to Know You: Hometown and Family , small group conversation
Getting to Know You: Hobbies, Interests and Occupation, pair and small group conversation
Introducing Japanese culture in English: Helping tourists and international people
Introducing Japanese culture in English: Your Hometown
Introducing Japanese culture in English: Food and other cultural items
Mid-term assessment and feedback using KU rubric, first half of class
Mid-term assessment and feedback using KU rubric, second half of class
Developing Eloquence: How to describe things (Pictures), pair and group work
Developing Eloquence: Translating familiar, day to day situations into English (Video), pair and group work
Developing Eloquence: Inference, interpretation, and the positives and negatives of assumption, pair and group work
Developing Eloquence: Review, pair and group work
General practice and review, feedback and exam preparation
Final course assessment 1: (KU rubric: keeping going, content, pronunciation, grammar/vocabulary) first half
Final course assessment 2: (KU rubric: keeping going, content, pronunciation, grammar/vocabulary)

prereq: This course is available to all first-year students and is a requirement for graduation.
prep:
Students should be prepared to review and practice the vocabulary and communication moves (e.g., asking follow‐up
questions) at home. They will be asked to do pre‐class pre‐writing and to record their compositions from time to time.
